description:
<title>Collect and Consolidate Simulated Edge-Node Metrics</title>

    <description>
        You are an edge-computing engineer validating a monitoring workflow before live rollout to hundreds of IoT devices.  
        A tiny simulator that continuously emits **static, deterministic** metrics has already been staged for you under:

            /home/user/iot_simulator/nodeA/metrics_feed
            /home/user/iot_simulator/nodeB/metrics_feed
            /home/user/iot_simulator/nodeC/metrics_feed

        Each *metrics_feed* file contains **exactly 12 lines** (one minute of data sampled every 5 s).  
        Every line is a single CSV record with this strict column order:

            Timestamp,CPU_USER,CPU_SYSTEM,CPU_IDLE,MEM_FREE_KB,DISK_READ_KBps,DISK_WRITE_KBps,NET_RX_KBps,NET_TX_KBps

        Timestamp format is the ISO-8601 form “YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss” (e.g. “2024-05-01T12:00:00”).

        Your objectives:

        1. Create a working directory called  
               /home/user/edge_monitoring  
           (make sure it exists before you place anything in it).

        2. Start **three parallel background capture jobs** that simultaneously read from the three *metrics_feed* files
           and write their data, untouched, into the following logs (one file per node):

               /home/user/edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeA.log
               /home/user/edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeB.log
               /home/user/edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeC.log

           • The logs must contain the 12 simulator lines *verbatim* and nothing else – no extra headers or blank lines.  
           • The capture jobs must terminate automatically after all 12 lines have been written.

        3. Merge the three per-node logs into a single CSV called  

               /home/user/edge_monitoring/all_nodes_metrics.csv

           Required format of this consolidated CSV:

               a) First row:  
                      Node,Timestamp,CPU_USER,CPU_SYSTEM,CPU_IDLE,MEM_FREE_KB,DISK_READ_KBps,DISK_WRITE_KBps,NET_RX_KBps,NET_TX_KBps

               b) Rows 2-37 (total 36 data rows):  
                  – Exactly 12 blocks, one per 5-second interval.  
                  – Within every block the rows must appear in the fixed order **NodeA, NodeB, NodeC**.  
                  – Therefore overall ordering is:  
                        2024-05-01T12:00:00 NodeA  
                        2024-05-01T12:00:00 NodeB  
                        2024-05-01T12:00:00 NodeC  
                        2024-05-01T12:00:05 NodeA  
                        … continuing until 2024-05-01T12:00:55 NodeC.

        4. Compute a compact human-readable report called  

               /home/user/edge_monitoring/summary.txt

           It must contain exactly these four lines (no extra whitespace):

               Samples processed: 12
               NodeA: CPU_USER=10.00, CPU_SYSTEM=5.00, CPU_IDLE=85.00
               NodeB: CPU_USER=15.00, CPU_SYSTEM=7.00, CPU_IDLE=78.00
               NodeC: CPU_USER=20.00, CPU_SYSTEM=9.00, CPU_IDLE=71.00

        5. Package the directory into a gzip-compressed tarball named  

               /home/user/edge_monitoring/edge_metrics_20240501.tar.gz

           The archive must contain exactly these four artefacts (relative paths preserved):  
               edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeA.log  
               edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeB.log  
               edge_monitoring/metrics_nodeC.log  
               edge_monitoring/all_nodes_metrics.csv  
               edge_monitoring/summary.txt

        When you are finished, the automated checker will inspect:

        • Existence of all required files.  
        • Exact number of lines, their order, and byte-for-byte content (case, commas, spaces, newline chars).  
        • Presence of the correctly-named tarball containing the precise files.

        Do NOT remove or modify the original simulator files.  
        You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities available in a typical Ubuntu installation.  
        Remember to run the three capture jobs in parallel and ensure they all finish cleanly before you proceed to later steps.
    </description>
name: task_000000_08c10891
